resultRaw.sql:
SELECT
  cs.course_id AS course_id,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS evaluations_count
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
GROUP BY cs.course_id
ORDER BY cs.course_id ASC;

issues: course_id values appear to be UUIDs (metadata sample). Using raw course_id is correct per SQL but not human-friendly — consider joining courses to show course names/titles.

The Vega-Lite encoding does not declare any aggregate — this is OK because the query returns pre-aggregated rows, but ensure the visualization layer treats the data as already-aggregated to avoid double-aggregation.

Color is mapped to evaluations_count (quantitative) with the legend disabled; this is redundant with the y-axis and adds little information — consider removing color encoding or enabling a legend if intended.
If there are many courses (rowCount=16 in metadata), the chart may become crowded; consider sorting bars (e.g., by evaluations_count) or limiting to top N courses for readability.

result.sql:
SELECT
  cs.course_id AS course_id,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS evaluations_count
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
GROUP BY cs.course_id
ORDER BY cs.course_id ASC;

issues: course_id values appear to be UUIDs (metadata sample). Using raw course_id is correct per SQL but not human-friendly — consider joining courses to show course names/titles.

The Vega-Lite encoding does not declare any aggregate — this is OK because the query returns pre-aggregated rows, but ensure the visualization layer treats the data as already-aggregated to avoid double-aggregation.

Color is mapped to evaluations_count (quantitative) with the legend disabled; this is redundant with the y-axis and adds little information — consider removing color encoding or enabling a legend if intended.
If there are many courses (rowCount=16 in metadata), the chart may become crowded; consider sorting bars (e.g., by evaluations_count) or limiting to top N courses for readability.
